binaries.title: fastfat.sys - FAT boot-sector (BPB) parameter computation reviewed across a cross-branch build pair; overflow validation present in both builds

changed_functions.name: FatSetupAllocationSupport (sub_1C0025258) / sub_140023300 (BPB parameter computation - computes FAT layout from boot sector fields)
key_changes: Reads on-disk boot sector (BPB) fields from the VCB: total sectors, sectors-per-FAT (FAT16 and FAT32), root-dir entries, num-FATs, sectors-per-cluster, bytes-per-sector, reserved sectors. Unpatched offsets 0x110-0x12c; patched offsets are +0x10 due to a VCB field insertion.

Performs chained multiplications and additions on these fields to compute FAT file size (fed to CcSetFileSizes) and the FAT-window pool allocation size (ExAllocatePoolWithTag(size = count * 0xC), tag 'FtaW').

Both builds guard the arithmetic with the same unsigned-overflow and bounds checks and reject malformed volumes via ExRaiseStatus(STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR, 0xC0000102): entry check reserved+numFATs*sectorsPerFAT wrap and bound; 32*rootEntries/bytesPerSector wrap and bound; FAT file-size wrap; allocation-count (rootDirBytes+0xFFFF) wrap.

Structural differences are recompilation artifacts: +0x10 VCB offset shift, the inlined log2 bit-scan loops factored into helper sub_140021EE0, and the entry wrap+bound checks merged into a single expression in the patched build. The set of rejected inputs is the same.
CcSetFileSizes and ExAllocatePoolWithTag('FtaW') are reached with the same guarded values in both builds.
changed_functions.security_notes: FatSetupAllocationSupport parses attacker-controllable FAT boot-sector fields and computes kernel allocation and cache-file sizes from chained arithmetic. In both the unpatched (10.0.20348.5020) and patched (10.0.28000.1896) builds the arithmetic feeding ExAllocatePoolWithTag('FtaW') and CcSetFileSizes is guarded by equivalent unsigned-overflow and bounds checks that reject malformed boot sectors with STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR. The validation is present in both builds, so this function makes no security-relevant change. The differences are recompilation artifacts across two servicing branches: a +0x10 VCB offset shift and factoring a log2 bit-scan loop into sub_140021EE0.

changed_functions.name: FatVerifyVolume (sub_1C0044490) / sub_140042848 (volume verification)
key_changes: Volume-verification routine. Changes are predominantly +0x10 offset shifts from the VCB field insertion and register renumbering.
No new security logic; differences are recompile-driven across the two servicing branches.

changed_functions.name: EfspHasPrivilege (sub_1C0007620) / sub_140007EF0 (privilege check)
key_changes: Walks the token privilege array via SeQueryInformationToken(TokenPrivileges) looking for LUID 0x12 (SeManageVolumePrivilege) with the enabled attribute set; loop restructured by compiler but logic identical.

changed_functions.name: EdpEnforcementLog_AutomaticEncryption (sub_1C005D144) / sub_140058A58 (EDP enforcement logging)
key_changes: Enterprise Data Protection enforcement logging for automatic encryption; trace/event formatting differs slightly across the recompile. No security impact.

changed_functions.name: IsExcludedExtension (sub_1C0009F5C) / sub_14000A774 (extension exclusion check)
key_changes: Checks whether a file extension is on an exclusion list; search loop restructured by compiler, comparison logic identical.

changed_functions.name: FatQueryFsAttributeInfo (sub_1C004EFD0) / sub_14004A3F8 (FS attribute query)
key_changes: Reports filesystem attribute/name information (FAT12/FAT16/FAT32). Offset shifts and minor flag-computation refactor; no security logic change.

security_findings.entry_point: FAT volume online path — boot-sector (BPB) fields parsed into the VCB are consumed by FatSetupAllocationSupport to compute the FAT layout, cache file size, and the FAT-window pool allocation.
call_chain: FAT volume brought online; boot-sector (BPB) fields parsed into the VCB
FatSetupAllocationSupport (sub_1C0025258) computes layout, validates arithmetic, calls CcSetFileSizes and ExAllocatePoolWithTag('FtaW')
security_findings.vulnerability_class: None identified in the diff — integer-overflow validation on BPB arithmetic is present in both builds (CWE-190 guards emitted in both)
security_findings.description: FatSetupAllocationSupport reads FAT boot-sector fields from the VCB (bytes-per-sector, reserved sectors, number of FATs, sectors-per-FAT for FAT16/FAT32, root-directory entries, sectors-per-cluster) and performs chained multiplication/addition to compute: (1) the FAT file size handed to CcSetFileSizes, and (2) the FAT-window pool allocation size for ExAllocatePoolWithTag (size = count * 0xC, count = (rootDirBytes + 0xFFFF) >> 16, tag 'FtaW' / 0x57746146). Both the unpatched (10.0.20348.5020) and patched (10.0.28000.1896) builds guard every one of these chains with unsigned-overflow and bounds checks and reject malformed boot sectors via ExRaiseStatus(STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR, 0xC0000102). Because the guards are present and semantically equivalent in both builds, this function makes no security-relevant change. The two builds differ only by recompilation artifacts: a +0x10 field insertion in the VCB that shifts struct offsets, and one inlined log2 bit-scan loop factored into helper sub_140021EE0.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: FatSetupAllocationSupport runs while a FAT volume is being brought online after its boot-sector fields are parsed into the VCB. A boot sector whose products overflow 32-bit arithmetic (for example numFATs * sectorsPerFAT wrapping, or the root-directory computation wrapping) takes one of the ExRaiseStatus(STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR) paths and the volume is rejected. This rejection behavior is the same on both builds; the overflow does not reach an undersized allocation on either.
security_findings.primitive: None. The FAT-window count feeding ExAllocatePoolWithTag('FtaW', 12*count) is guarded by an explicit wrap check ((rootDirBytes+0xFFFF) wrap -> reject), and the FAT file size feeding CcSetFileSizes is guarded by its own wrap check, in both builds.
security_findings.assembly:
Unpatched FatSetupAllocationSupport starts at 0x1C0025258 in fastfat_unpatched.sys. Verified call/branch sites:
  0x1C0025315: call ExRaiseStatus            (STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR on entry-check failure)
  0x1C0025332: imul r11d, r9d                (numFATs * sectorsPerFAT, followed by wrap check)
  0x1C00256A0: call CcSetFileSizes           (FileSize from guarded arithmetic)
  0x1C00256E2: call ExRaiseStatus            (STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR on allocation-count wrap)
  0x1C0025709: call ExAllocatePoolWithTag    (size = 12*count, tag 'FtaW' 0x57746146)
  0x1C002580C: call ExRaiseStatus            (STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR)
The patched build sub_140023300 emits the same sequence with +0x10 offsets and register reallocation; the inlined log2 bit-scan loops (guard KeBugCheckEx(0x23,0x21309,...)) are moved into helper sub_140021EE0 (guard code 0x2131B).

breakpoints.why: Entry to the parameter computation. rcx = context (IrpContext), rdx = VCB. Parsed BPB fields sit at fixed offsets from the VCB base (unpatched: +0x110 bytes-per-sector, +0x112 sectors-per-cluster, +0x114 reserved sectors, +0x116 numFATs, +0x118 root-directory entries, +0x11A total sectors (16-bit), +0x11E sectors-per-FAT (16-bit), +0x12C sectors-per-FAT (32-bit)).
FAT-window allocation. r8 (size) = 12 * count, r9 (tag) = 0x57746146 ('FtaW'). count = (rootDirBytes+0xFFFF)>>16 is guarded by a wrap check, so it cannot reach here wrapped to a small value.
FileSizes.FileSize is computed from arithmetic guarded by a wrap check (if (v39 < bytesPerSector*reserved) reject).
A malformed BPB that overflows any guarded chain lands on one of these and the volume is rejected with 0xC0000102.

watch: rdx+0x110 (bytes_per_sector), rdx+0x112 (sectors_per_cluster), rdx+0x114 (reserved_sectors), rdx+0x116 (numFATs), rdx+0x118 (root_dir_entries), rdx+0x11A (total_sectors_16), rdx+0x11E (sectors_per_fat_16), rdx+0x12C (sectors_per_fat_32)
reserved + numFATs*sectorsPerFAT — followed by unsigned wrap check (reject on wrap)
(rootDirBytes+0xFFFF) — followed by unsigned wrap check before >>16 and 12*count allocation
key_offsets: 0x1c0025332 — imul r11d, r9d (numFATs * sectorsPerFAT), guarded by following wrap check
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Prepare a FAT16/FAT32 boot sector with BPB fields whose products overflow 32-bit arithmetic. 2. Bring the volume online so its boot sector is parsed into the VCB and FatSetupAllocationSupport runs. 3. Break on the entry and observe which guard fires; overflowing inputs route to ExRaiseStatus(STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: A boot sector engineered to overflow the guarded arithmetic causes the volume to be rejected with STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR (0xC0000102) rather than producing an undersized allocation. The same outcome occurs on both builds.
debugging_aids.notes: The VCB layout differs between the two builds by a +0x10 field insertion; the field offsets above are for the unpatched (10.0.20348.5020) build — add 0x10 for the patched (10.0.28000.1896) build. Symbols in the patched binary are stripped, so its functions appear as sub_<addr>.

summary: Across a cross-branch build pair (unpatched fastfat.sys 10.0.20348.5020 vs patched 10.0.28000.1896), the FAT boot-sector (BPB) parameter computation function FatSetupAllocationSupport (sub_1C0025258, paired with sub_140023300 in the patched build) was reviewed for the integer-overflow-to-undersized-pool-allocation pattern (CWE-190/CWE-787). The function reads attacker-controllable on-disk FAT metadata (numFATs, sectorsPerFAT, bytesPerSector, root-directory entries, reserved/total sectors) and performs chained multiplication/addition to compute the FAT file size fed to CcSetFileSizes and the FAT-window pool allocation size fed to ExAllocatePoolWithTag (tag 'FtaW'). Both builds guard every one of these chains with equivalent unsigned-overflow and bounds checks and reject malformed boot sectors via ExRaiseStatus(STATUS_FILE_CORRUPT_ERROR, 0xC0000102). Because the validation is present and semantically equivalent in both builds, this function makes no security-relevant change. The two builds differ only by recompilation artifacts: a +0x10 VCB field insertion that shifts struct offsets, and factoring an inlined log2 bit-scan loop into helper sub_140021EE0. The KB metadata records no CVE attributed to fastfat.sys. Verified offsets: ExRaiseStatus rejection at 0x1C0025315/0x1C00256E2/0x1C002580C, CcSetFileSizes at 0x1C00256A0, ExAllocatePoolWithTag('FtaW') at 0x1C0025709.
